 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3rgx","title":"[SC] Host verification pipeline + policy gate","description":"# [SC] Host verification pipeline + policy gate

## Goal
Verify attestation + SBOM before connector install/upgrade and enforce policy gates in fcp-host with deterministic evidence bundles.

## Deliverables
- Verification pipeline steps:
  - Parse manifest + binary digest.
  - Verify attestation signatures and bind to artifact digest.
  - Verify SBOM signature and hash match.
  - Enforce policy (require_attestation, min_slsa_level, trusted_builders, rekor_required, allow_unsigned).
  - Produce evidence bundle (verification steps, hashes, trust roots, timestamps).
- Policy config surface in host (zone or global).
- Evidence bundle stored as mesh object (deterministic, redacted).

## Design Notes
- Fail closed by default; allow_unsigned must be explicit and scoped to dev.
- Verification must be deterministic (ordering, canonical encoding, stable hashes).
- Redact secrets and opaque tokens in logs/evidence.
- Cache verified artifacts for offline installs; cache keyed by digest.

## Tests
- Unit tests for each verification rule and error code mapping.
- Integration tests with test connectors + signed/unsigned fixtures.
- Property tests for determinism (same inputs -> same bundle hash).
- Structured JSON logging for each verification step.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Missing/invalid attestations block install unless policy allows.
- Evidence bundle is deterministic, audit-ready, and redacted.
- Clear, actionable error codes for each failure case.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3tja","title":"Mesh Trace Capture + Replay Debugger","description":"# Mesh Trace Capture + Replay Debugger

## Goal
Enable deterministic capture and replay of mesh traffic for debugging and incident analysis.

## Why
- Reproduce complex mesh failures offline.
- Provide evidence bundles for audits and regressions.
- Improve developer velocity for hard-to-debug issues.

## Scope
- Trace format with redaction policy.
- Capture hooks in MeshNode (routing/admission/leases).
- Replay engine + CLI with deterministic playback.

## Non-goals
- Full production tracing UI; CLI only for now.

## Deliverables
- Trace schema + canonical encoding rules.
- Redaction policy (PII, secrets, tokens).
- MeshNode capture with sampling and size caps.
- Replay engine + `fcp trace replay` CLI.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E script with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Captures can be replayed to reproduce routing/admission decisions.
- Sensitive fields are redacted or encrypted.
- Deterministic outputs for identical traces.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3z2","title":"fcp.spotify: Library Management","description":"# fcp.spotify: Library Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe access to the user’s library:
- list saved tracks/albums/shows
- save/unsave items

Library writes modify user state and must be auditable.

## Scope
- Get saved tracks/albums/shows/episodes (pagination).
- Save/unsave items.
- Check whether items are saved.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.library.read`
- `spotify.library.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include item ids and action
  - never log track names or personal context

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.library.tracks.list`
  - `spotify.library.tracks.save`
  - `spotify.library.tracks.remove`
  - `spotify.library.albums.list`
  - `spotify.library.albums.save/remove`
  - `spotify.library.shows.list/save/remove`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Library operations are reliable, safe, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-41uql","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: MeshSessionHello transcript sign/verify field-binding fuzz","description":"fcp_protocol::session::MeshSessionHello::sign + verify (session.rs:374-
390) is the Ed25519 transcript signature that proves a hello is
genuine. The transcript is:
  b"FCP2-HELLO-V1" || from || to || eph_pubkey || nonce || cookie ||
  timestamp || suites || transport_limits
(canonical-CBOR encoded per field, see transcript_bytes session.rs:356-
367).

This is the hello's authentication binding — distinct from:
  - verify_cookie / compute_cookie (674lv): the stateless HMAC gate on
    a cookie key (DoS-prevention layer)
  - verify_session_mac (session_metamorphic): the per-frame MAC over
    derived session keys
  - NodeKeyAttestation::verify (xedeu): owner-issued attestation over
    node identity

A regression in the hello transcript would let an attacker forge or
pivot a captured hello — the responder accepts under a different
(from, to, eph_pubkey, ...) tuple than was signed. None of the existing
fuzz targets exercise the per-field binding of the hello transcript.

Properties asserted:

  1. **Round-trip**: sign(key, h) → verify(key.pub, h) returns Ok.
  2. **Per-field binding (×7)**: bit-flipping any one of
     (from, to, eph_pubkey, nonce, cookie, timestamp, suites,
     transport_limits) MUST cause verify to return InvalidSignature.
  3. **Wrong-key rejection**: a hello signed under key A MUST NOT
     verify under any different key B.
  4. **MissingSignature on unsigned**: a hello with signature=None
     MUST yield SessionError::MissingSignature on verify.

  Once-gated regression anchors:
    (a) Domain-tag binding via timestamp: a hello signed at t=1 MUST
        NOT verify at t=2 (timestamp dropped from transcript would
        let captured hellos replay forever — same regression class
        as the verify_cookie 674lv anchor, but for the inner
        transcript path).
    (b) eph_pubkey binding: hello signed with eph_pubkey A MUST NOT
        verify with eph_pubkey B (mid-handshake ephemeral swap-in
        surface, but at the inner-transcript layer).

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_transcript_binding.rs
  - 4 properties + 2 once-gated anchors above.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:26:46.117404Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:28:21.487857Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:28:21.487392Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3095,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-41uql","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit ff327edd. fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_transcript_binding.rs — 4 properties (round-trip, per-field binding ×7 with no-op-mutation guard, wrong-key rejection, MissingSignature on unsigned) + 2 once-gated anchors for timestamp binding (parallel to 674lv cookie anchor) and eph_pubkey binding at the transcript layer. Distinct from verify_cookie/verify_session_mac/NodeKeyAttestation surfaces. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:28:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-58x","title":"fcp.jira: Sprint and board management","description":"# fcp.jira: Sprint and board management (FCP2)

## Goal
Implement Jira Agile operations for **boards** and **sprints** so agents can manage sprint workflows safely under FCP2 capability + approval enforcement.

This bead is specifically about Jira Software (Agile) surfaces (boards, sprints, backlog), not generic issue CRUD.

## Scope
### Read operations (low risk)
- List boards visible to the principal.
- Get board configuration (type, filters, location).
- List sprints for a board; get sprint metadata.
- List issues in a sprint/backlog (pagination; stable ordering).

### Write operations (medium/high risk)
- Create sprint (board-scoped).
- Start / complete sprint.
- Move issues into/out of sprint and backlog.

### Constraints
- Must work in both Jira Cloud and Server/DC (via `flywheel_connectors-3a9`).
- Must respect project permissions and Jira Software license availability.

## Capability model (illustrative)
Capability IDs MUST NOT encode hosts/ports.
- `jira.boards.read`
- `jira.sprints.read`
- `jira.sprints.write` (create/start/complete)
- `jira.sprints.assign_issues` (move issues)

Danger/approval guidance:
- Starting/completing sprints should be **approval-gated** by policy in many orgs.
  - Emit DecisionReceipt reason codes that are specific (e.g., `JIRA_SPRINT_START_REQUIRES_APPROVAL`).

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to the Jira base hostname.
- For write ops: emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
  - board_id / sprint_id / issue_keys (IDs only; no secret data)
  - request correlation id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.board.list`
  - `jira.board.get`
  - `jira.sprint.list`
  - `jira.sprint.get`
  - `jira.sprint.create`
  - `jira.sprint.start`
  - `jira.sprint.complete`
  - `jira.sprint.issues`
  - `jira.sprint.move_issues`
  - `jira.backlog.move_issues`
- Robust pagination helpers (bounded memory; streaming-friendly).
- Clear error mapping when Jira Software APIs are unavailable.

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Mock fixtures for Agile endpoints:
  - board listing and sprint listing
  - start/complete sprint happy paths
  - permission denied
  - missing Jira Software (feature not enabled)
  - rate-limit + retry/backoff

### Compliance
- Ensure capability-gating is enforced for each operation.
- Ensure write operations produce receipts/audit events.

## Acceptance criteria
- A mock-only test suite covers both Cloud and Server/DC variants.
- All write operations are capability + (policy-configurable) approval gated.
- No assumptions about “global boards”; all operations are explicitly scoped.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l25","title":"fcp.terraform: Resource import for existing infrastructure","description":"# fcp.terraform: Resource import for existing infrastructure (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow controlled `terraform import` so agents can bring existing infrastructure under state management.

Import mutates state and can be risky; require approval gating.

## Scope
- Import a resource into state with explicit address + provider id.
- Validate inputs and prevent injection into CLI arguments.
- Emit receipts/audit for the import action.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.import` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile restricts filesystem + process execution.
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless provider APIs are explicitly allowed.
- Approval gating recommended.

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `terraform.import`
- Input validation:
  - strict address grammar
  - strict provider id format

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim for:
  - arg construction
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Imports are mechanically authorized, safe, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","title":"TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests (Frame Parsing, AEAD, MAC Verification)","description":"# TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove FCPS/FCPC framing, parsing, and crypto verification are correct and bounded under adversarial inputs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for wire protocol frame parsing and cryptographic verification.

## FCPS (Symbol Plane) Tests

### Frame Parsing
- [ ] Valid frame parses correctly
- [ ] Magic bytes validation ("FCPS")
- [ ] Version field bounds checking
- [ ] Flag combinations parsed correctly
- [ ] Symbol count matches actual symbols
- [ ] Total payload length matches content
- [ ] Zone ID hash is 32 bytes exactly
- [ ] Object ID is 32 bytes exactly

### Symbol Record Parsing
- [ ] ESI (encoding symbol identifier) extraction
- [ ] K parameter extraction
- [ ] Data length matches symbol_size
- [ ] Auth tag is 16 bytes exactly

### Datagram Envelope
- [ ] Session ID extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] Sequence number parsing (u64 LE)
- [ ] MAC extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] MAC verification success with correct key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with wrong key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with tampered frame
- [ ] Direction byte in MAC context

### Per-Symbol AEAD
- [ ] Sender key derivation matches spec
- [ ] Nonce derivation: frame_seq || esi
- [ ] AAD binding includes all required fields
- [ ] Decrypt success with correct key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with tampered ciphertext
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong AAD

### Frame Flags
- [ ] REQUIRES_ACK flag handling
- [ ] COMPRESSED flag handling
- [ ] ENCRYPTED flag handling
- [ ] STREAMING + STREAM_END combination
- [ ] HAS_CAP_TOKEN flag handling
- [ ] ZONE_CROSSING flag handling
- [ ] CONTROL_PLANE flag handling
- [ ] Invalid flag combinations rejected

## FCPC (Control Plane) Tests

### Frame Structure
- [ ] Canonical CBOR envelope parsing
- [ ] Object type discrimination
- [ ] Length prefix validation
- [ ] Backpressure frame parsing

### Control Objects
- [ ] ControlPlaneObject type dispatch
- [ ] Session control messages
- [ ] Capability objects
- [ ] Revocation objects
- [ ] Checkpoint objects

## DoS Resistance Tests
- [ ] Oversized frame rejected
- [ ] Invalid symbol count rejected
- [ ] Frame exceeding MTU rejected
- [ ] Memory bounds on malformed input

## Golden Vectors
Each test category needs:
- Valid frame bytes (hex)
- Expected parsed structure
- Invalid frame bytes that MUST be rejected

## Fuzz Targets
- FCPS frame parsing
- FCPC frame parsing
- Symbol record parsing
- Datagram envelope parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "protocol": "fcps|fcpc",
  "operation": "parse|verify|encode",
  "frame_size": 1234,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "error_code": "optional"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All parsing paths have test coverage
- DoS protections verified
- Golden vectors match exactly
- Fuzz targets find no crashes

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mhiv","title":"[FCP2] Computation Migration: MigratableComputation, Checkpoint Transfer, Execution Lease Handoff","description":"# [FCP2] Computation Migration (SUPERSEDED)

This bead is **superseded** by `flywheel_connectors-6o25.4`, which is the canonical Full Profile Computation Migration plan.

---

## Original Content (kept for history)

## Goal
Implement the computation migration protocol from V2 spec section 16, enabling:
- MigratableComputation state management
- Checkpoint creation and transfer as symbols
- Execution lease ownership transfer during migration
- Resume on target device from checkpoint

## Normative Requirements (from FCP Specification V2 section 16)
- Checkpoints MUST be content-addressed objects distributed via symbols
- Execution leases MUST be transferred before resumption to prevent duplicate execution
- Migration state transitions: Running -> Suspended -> (transfer) -> Running

## Implementation Details

### MigratableComputation Schema
- computation_id: ObjectId
- capability: MeshCapability
- state: ComputationState (Running/Suspended/Completed/Failed)
- current_device: TailscaleNodeId

### Migration Protocol
1. Checkpoint current state (serialize + store)
2. Distribute checkpoint as symbols to target
3. Send migration request to target node
4. Transfer execution lease ownership (prevents duplicate execution)
5. Target reconstructs checkpoint and resumes

## Dependencies
- Requires Leases (1n78.34) for execution lease transfer
- Requires Stores (1n78.15) for checkpoint storage
- Requires MeshNode (1n78.17) for migration request routing

## Tests
- Unit tests: checkpoint creation/restoration, state transitions
- Integration tests: full migration cycle between nodes
- Adversarial tests: migration during network partition, duplicate execution prevention

## Acceptance Criteria
- Computation can migrate between devices without duplicate execution
- Checkpoint includes all necessary state for resume
- Migration is audited and produces audit events

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mkj","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Automation management (create, edit, enable/disable)","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Automation management (create, edit, enable/disable) (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage Home Assistant automations safely:
- list automations
- enable/disable
- (optional) create/update/delete automations

Automation changes can have broad side effects; treat as dangerous.

## Scope
### Read
- List automations and their enabled/disabled state.

### Write (dangerous)
- Enable/disable automation.
- Optional: create/update/delete automation definitions.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.automations.read`
- `ha.automations.write` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Approval gating recommended for any automation writes.
- Receipts + audit events include automation id + action.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.automation.list`
  - `ha.automation.enable`
  - `ha.automation.disable`
  - Optional: `ha.automation.create/update/delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - enable/disable flows
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Automation changes are mechanically authorized and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-p5ams","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: HelloReplayWindow state-machine fuzz (FIFO eviction + (from,nonce) keying)","description":"fcp_protocol::session::HelloReplayWindow (session.rs:553-599) is the
bounded FIFO replay-protection window for handshake hellos. The
`verify_hello_attested_with_replay` path (session.rs:650-662) gates new
sessions on `check_and_update` returning true; a regression in the
window's bookkeeping would either:
  - admit a duplicate (from, nonce) that's still within the active
    window → handshake-replay attack surface re-opened.
  - evict and re-admit prematurely → still a replay window leak.
  - cap-bypass (seen.len() exceeds capacity) → DoS amplification surface
    if attackers spray distinct (from, nonce) tuples.

Existing fcp-protocol fuzz coverage in this area:
  - session_metamorphic        — verify_session_mac binding MRs
  - session_cookie_binding     — verify_cookie field-binding (674lv)
  - derive_session_keys        — KDF binding MRs
  - session_transcript / session — handshake decode

NOT covered: HelloReplayWindow's state-machine invariants.

Properties asserted (state-machine MRs):

  1. **First-time accept**: a hello not in the window MUST be accepted
     by check_and_update.
  2. **Immediate-replay reject**: re-inserting the SAME hello MUST be
     rejected; check returns false in lockstep.
  3. **check ⇔ check_and_update agreement**: if check returns false,
     check_and_update MUST also return false; if check returns true,
     check_and_update MUST return true on the first call.
  4. **FIFO eviction with re-admit**: after capacity+N unique inserts
     (N ≥ 1), the OLDEST key MUST have been evicted, and a re-insert
     of that key MUST be accepted again (was outside the window).
  5. **Capacity bound enforcement**: at all times seen.len() and
     order.len() are ≤ capacity.
  6. **(from, nonce) keying**: two hellos differing in `from` only
     MUST be tracked independently (both accepted on first insert);
     two hellos differing in `nonce` only MUST also be tracked
     independently. A regression that hashed only one field would
     surface here.

Note: HelloReplayWindow.seen / .order are private; we observe state
indirectly via check + check_and_update return values, which is how
the security boundary is actually exposed to callers.

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_replay_window.rs
  - 6 properties + once-gated regression anchors for (a) FIFO
    eviction at capacity boundary and (b) (from, nonce) keying.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:14:18.986114Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:15:43.208793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:15:43.208330Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3084,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p5ams","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit ecb82520. fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_replay_window.rs — 4 properties driven by an external VecDeque FIFO model (first-time accept, immediate-replay reject, check⇔check_and_update agreement against the model on every op, (from,nonce) keying parity) + once-gated anchors for capacity-boundary FIFO eviction (h0..h3 with capacity=3) and same-nonce-different-from / same-from-different-nonce keying. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:15:42Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yr0","title":"fcp.figma: Comment thread management","description":"# fcp.figma: Comment thread management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to manage Figma comment threads for design review workflows:
- list comments
- create comment
- reply
- resolve/unresolve

## Scope
### Read
- List comments for a file (pagination).

### Write (sensitive)
- Create comment on a node.
- Reply to a thread.
- Resolve/unresolve threads.
- Delete comment (dangerous; optional).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.comments.read`
- `figma.comments.write` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `figma.comments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Comments contain human text and may contain sensitive info:
  - treat as tainted
  - never log raw comment bodies
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include file key + comment id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.comment.list`
  - `figma.comment.create`
  - `figma.comment.reply`
  - `figma.comment.resolve`
  - Optional: `figma.comment.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - write flows + audit/receipt
  - permission denied
  - log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Comment operations are safe, auditable, and do not leak content into logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yu0l5","title":"[PERF] fcp-raptorq::add_symbol: retries full decode on every post-K symbol","description":"crates/fcp-raptorq/src/decode.rs:119-145 and 151-234. Once received_count() >= K, add_symbol() calls try_reconstruct() on every newly arrived symbol. try_reconstruct() rebuilds decoder state, clones every buffered symbol into ReceivedSymbol rows, and allocates zero-filled virtual padding rows for K..K' on each attempt. If a decode needs K+R symbols, the total work becomes roughly sum_{i=K}^{K+R} O(i * symbol_size) instead of one decode plus bounded retries, which is a throughput cliff on lossy links. Proposed improvement: remember the last attempted symbol count / rank progress and only retry on meaningful new information (for example repair rows or a backoff schedule), or keep an incremental decoder state instead of rebuilding the full equation set on every symbol after K.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:59.607724Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:59.404402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:59.404086Z","close_reason":"Added post-K retry coalescer to RaptorQDecoder::add_symbol (br-yu0l5). New state: last_attempted_at_count + post_k_retry_step (starts at 1, doubles on InsufficientSymbols, cap MAX_POST_K_RETRY_STEP=32). Decode attempts now fire only at (a) first K-crossover, (b) every repair-symbol arrival (ESI >= K; fresh LT equation), and (c) when received_count >= last_attempted + post_k_retry_step. Non-repair symbols between backoff ticks no longer trigger full try_reconstruct() rebuilds, eliminating the sum_{i=K}^{K+R} O(i * symbol_size) throughput cliff described in the bead. Regression tests: 5 new covering first crossover, repair-always-fires, source-only skip, threshold-reached trigger, backoff saturation arithmetic, plus round_trip_decode_after_backoff_coalesce_preserves_correctness (encode + decode a 640-byte payload via encoder.encode_all() and assert the full payload reconstructs cleanly — correctness regression harness). Verified: 756 fcp-raptorq lib tests pass (was 750 + 6 new). Pre-existing 4 failures (decoder_*_duplicate / slot_cap / cancellation) are unrelated and reproduce on HEAD without these changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

### Batching & Deduplication

- [ ] Query deduplication
- [ ] Cache integration

### Schema Introspection
- [ ] Schema fetching
